title: The UK and International Institutions
overview: The United Kingdom plays an active part in international affairs. It works with other countries to promote peace, security, human rights, and economic progress. Through international organisations like the Commonwealth, United Nations, NATO, and Council of Europe, Britain helps tackle global challenges and supports cooperation.

study_sections.heading: Commonwealth
study_sections.content: Commonwealth: voluntary association of 56 independent countries, most former British Empire. British Monarch is ceremonial Head. Members work together to promote democracy, human rights, development. Examples: Canada, Australia, India, Nigeria, New Zealand, South Africa.

study_sections.heading: United Nations
study_sections.content: UN founded 1945 after Second World War to maintain peace and promote cooperation. UK is one of 5 permanent members of UN Security Council (with USA, France, China, Russia). UK contributes troops and aid to UN peacekeeping and humanitarian missions. HQ in New York.

study_sections.heading: NATO and Council of Europe
study_sections.content: NATO formed 1949 by Western allies for mutual defence. If one member attacked, others help defend. UK is founding member and leading contributor. Council of Europe founded 1949, separate from EU. Works to protect human rights, democracy, rule of law across Europe. European Court of Human Rights in Strasbourg enforces principles.

study_sections.heading: Other Organizations
study_sections.content: European Union: UK was member 1973-2020 (Brexit). Now maintains trade and security cooperation under new agreements. UK belongs to WTO, G7, G20 coordinating global trade and economic policy. Through these institutions, Britain promotes peace, free trade, shared values worldwide.

key_facts: British Monarch: ceremonial Head of Commonwealth
Commonwealth promotes: democracy, human rights, development
UK: UN Security Council permanent member (with USA, France, China, Russia)
Council of Europe: protects human rights & democracy
European Court of Human Rights: Strasbourg
UK values in foreign policy: peace, democracy, cooperation
UK aims: global peace & shared progress
memory_hook: UK international as global network: Center node (UK), Main connections: Commonwealth (family tree—56 countries, Monarch at top), UN (peacekeepers—Security Council seat), NATO (shield—mutual defence since 1949), Council of Europe (scales—human rights in Strasbourg). Former connection: EU (1973-2020). Trade links: WTO, G7. All wires carry same signal: democracy, peace, cooperation!

questions.prompt: European Court of Human Rights based?
Britain's aim in joining international institutions?

flashcards: Five permanent UN Security Council members
Group of independent countries working for peace & democracy
UK role in UN Security Council

supportive_messages.high_score: Excellent! You understand UK global role brilliantly! 🌍
